validated_reasoning: The study investigates various aspects of the tourist gaze in Bangkok, focusing on the topics of gaze, the influence of Western-centric imagery, and the portrayal of gaze dichotomies through photographs. This indicates an exploration of patterns and themes rather than formal hypothesis testing or prediction, aligning with the exploratory research type [1], [2].

validated_bibliography.direct_quote: The study proposes to investigate: - (1) the focus (topics) of the gaze and the tourism space that different tourist groups and locals view in Bangkok; - (2) the influence that the Western-centric imagery has upon the Western tourist groups; - (3) the extent to which the gaze dichotomies have been portrayed from the photographs of tourists.

validated_bibliography.section: 3.4. Visual content analysis (VCA) of the tourist gaze
validated_bibliography.direct_quote: The emphasis of VCA in this study is to investigate the dichotomy of gazes; photos from the five topics (gazes) were selected reflecting the sociological view of gazer-gazee dichotomies.

audit_commentary: All claims in the reasoning explanation are cited, and citation numbers match the bibliography entries. No uncited claims are present, and the evidence supports the classification as exploratory. The classification does not rely on machine learning or regression methods, thus no misclassification is detected.
